title: MARACE: Interaction Race Detection via Happens-Before-Aware Abstract Interpretation for Multi-Agent RL Safety Verification

claims.claim: An interaction race with respect to safety predicate φ is a triple (e, e', s) where: (i) e and e' are events of distinct agents, (ii) e and e' are concurrent, (iii) there exist two HB-consistent schedules σ₁, σ₂ such that e precedes e' in σ₁ and e' precedes e in σ₂, and the system is safe under σ₁ but unsafe under σ₂.

evidence.description: InteractionRace dataclass (line 241) encodes the race triple with fields for events, HB inconsistency, race condition, witness schedules, and absence certificates
ScheduleEvent (line 46), HBInconsistency (line 72), RaceCondition (line 115), and RaceWitness (line 154) encode the constituent parts of the definition
Happens-before graph and concurrency relation implementation used to determine concurrent events
Event data structures with agent identity and timing for multi-agent executions
test_race_creation, test_race_condition, test_race_witness, test_hb_inconsistency verify all components of the interaction race definition

claims.claim: There exists a multi-agent execution M with safety predicate φ such that each agent i individually satisfies φ restricted to its local state projection, yet M contains an interaction race with respect to φ.

evidence.description: Highway intersection environment implements the two-agent counter-example from the proof (agents with constant velocity toward origin, separation safety predicate)
InteractionRace with RaceWitness containing two schedules (σ₁ where A acts first, σ₂ simultaneous) demonstrating the race
test_race_absence and test_race_witness test construction of the witnessing schedules
Tests verifying that the highway environment produces races invisible to per-agent analysis
highway_intersection results with 2-agent configuration demonstrate the non-projectability witness concretely

claims.claim: For Lipschitz-continuous policies where the race manifold has codimension k ≥ 1, the number of uniform random schedules needed to detect a race with probability ≥ 1−δ is Ω((L/ε)^k ln(1/δ)).

evidence.description: ImportanceWeights and EffectiveSampleSize classes (lines 37, 70) implement the IS estimator that overcomes the intractability of uniform sampling
SpectralNormComputation (line 96) and LayerLipschitz (line 230) compute the Lipschitz constant L used in the bound
Schedule space representation used to characterize the codimension of the race manifold
test_importance_weights, test_effective_sample_size verify IS overcomes uniform sampling intractability

claims.claim: The interaction race detection problem for general Lipschitz-continuous policies is PSPACE-hard.

evidence.description: Proof sketch (lines 287-296) via reduction from reachability in bounded one-counter automata. Full reduction in Appendix app:pspace-proof
SingleAgentPWLVerifier (line 177) implements the tractable subcase (PWL policies), motivating the hardness result by showing the general case requires the PSPACE reduction

claims.claim: For piecewise-linear policies (e.g., ReLU networks) with at most W parameters and L layers, and linear safety predicates, the interaction race detection problem is solvable in time O(n² · W² · L).

evidence.description: SingleAgentPWLVerifier.enumerate_regions (line 227) partitions input space into linear regions; check_safety_violation (line 121 in AffineRegion) reduces race detection to linear feasibility per region
LinearTransfer (line 124) and ReLUTransfer (line 149) implement exact affine and piecewise-linear abstract transfer functions
Zonotope.affine_transform (line 197) provides exact propagation through linear layers in O(W²) per layer
test_soundness_linear, test_soundness_relu verify soundness of PWL transfer functions
test_affine_transform, test_affine_transform_dimension_change verify exact affine propagation

claims.claim: For a multi-agent system with ReLU network policies, each having L layers and at most W neurons per layer, the HB-aware abstract interpretation terminates in O(L · n) propagation steps and produces a sound over-approximation. The total generator count is bounded by O(n · L · W).

evidence.description: FixpointEngine.compute (line 294) implements the fixpoint iteration with bounded ascending chain (BoundedAscendingChain, line 116) that terminates in O(L·n) steps
ReLUTransfer.apply (line 167) introduces at most W fresh generators per layer via parallelotope relaxation, bounding total generators
ConvergenceChecker (line 70) monitors convergence; WideningStrategy (line 36) ensures termination via widening
HBConstraintSet (line 177) propagates HB constraints that tighten generator bounds at each step
test_stable_fixpoint, test_linear_fixpoint, test_fixpoint_with_widening verify convergence and termination
test_relu_preserves_dimension, test_chain_soundness verify generator count bounds and soundness

claims.claim: If MARACE reports no interaction race, then for all HB-consistent schedules σ and all initial states s₀ in the analyzed region, the safety predicate φ holds: φ(reach(σ, s₀)) = 1.

evidence.description: Full analysis pipeline (1060 lines) chains all stages: trace collection, HB construction, decomposition, abstract interpretation, MCTS search, probability estimation
FixpointEngine produces sound over-approximation of reachable states (Theorem 5 dependency)
HB constraints eliminate only physically infeasible schedules (Lemma 1 dependency)
Compositional decomposition with SoundnessProof (line 138) chain verifying assume-guarantee rule (Theorem 9 dependency)
MCTS search explores all non-pruned scheduling decisions
test_pipeline_creation, test_pipeline_has_run_and_summary verify end-to-end pipeline correctness
Integration tests verify soundness across the full pipeline

claims.claim: MARACE achieves recall of 1.00 across all configurations (highway intersection 2–4 agents, warehouse 4–8 agents), confirming no true interaction race is missed.

evidence.description: highway_intersection and warehouse_corridor result entries with recall=1.00 for all agent counts
Experiment runner that produces the detection results in Table 1
Recall, precision, FPR, and F1 metric computation
Experiment harness that runs benchmarks and collects metrics
Highway intersection benchmark environment
Warehouse corridor benchmark environment

claims.claim: Fitting a power law t = a · n^b yields exponent b = 0.93 (a = 0.0049, R² = 0.76), confirming near-linear scaling from 2 to 10 agents. All configurations complete in under 50ms.

evidence.description: scalability and scalability_fit entries with timing data for 2–10 agents and fitted power-law parameters
Scalability experiment that varies agent count and measures wall-clock time
InteractionGraph (line 63) enables compositional decomposition that prevents combinatorial explosion
Graph partitioning that decomposes agents into independent groups for parallel analysis

claims.claim: Ablation study (4-agent highway): HB pruning reduces MCTS from 201 nodes to 1 node (201× reduction). HB pruning yields tighter safety margin (0.305 vs 0.228). Compositional decomposition has minimal impact in this scenario.

evidence.description: ablation entry with 4 configurations (±decomposition, ±HB pruning) showing MCTS nodes, pruned count, safety margin, and time
AbstractPruner (line 100) and PruningStatistics (line 41) implement HB-aware pruning with statistics tracking
MCTS search tree (1003 lines) with configurable pruning that can be toggled for ablation
Compositional decomposition that can be toggled for ablation
test_safety_margin_pruner, test_composite_pruner, test_no_pruning_without_zonotope verify pruning behavior

claims.claim: Self-normalized concentration bound: P[|p̂_R^IS − p_R| > ε] ≤ 2 exp(−N_eff · ε² / (2M²)) where N_eff = (Σw_j)² / Σw_j² is the effective sample size and M bounds max w_j / w̄.

evidence.description: SelfNormalizedBound (line 175) implements the concentration bound with effective sample size monitoring (1659 lines total)
EmpiricalBernsteinBound (line 342) provides the Bernstein-type inequality used in the proof
EffectiveSampleSize.compute (line 80) computes N_eff = (Σw_j)²/Σw_j²; ConfidenceInterval.from_importance_samples (line 137) applies the bound
test_ci_width_decreases_with_n, test_ci_coverage_nominal, test_effective_sample_size verify the concentration bound properties
test_confidence_interval, test_hoeffding_bound, test_bernstein_bound verify IS confidence intervals

claims.claim: Calibration convergence: as calibration traces K → ∞, the empirical Lipschitz estimate L̂_K satisfies L̂_K ≤ Lip(π) ≤ L̂_K + O(√(log K / K)) with probability ≥ 1 − 1/K. This is a Banach fixed-point convergence result.

evidence.description: BanachFixedPointTheorem (line 161) implements contraction verification, iteration, guaranteed_iterations, and convergence certificates (1587 lines)
FixedPointCertificate (line 57) with verify, a_priori_bound, a_posteriori_bound methods for machine-checkable convergence proofs
SpectralNormComputation (line 96) computes per-layer spectral norms; ReLULipschitz (line 328) and LayerLipschitz (line 230) compute the Lipschitz constant
test_contraction_map_half, test_contraction_detected, test_certificate_verify, test_a_priori_bound_finite, test_verify_correct_fixed_point verify the Banach fixed-point theorem implementation
Tests for the LipSDP tighter Lipschitz bounds

claims.claim: Zonotope reduction error bound: Z ⊆ Z' ⊆ Z ⊕ B_∞(ε_red) where ε_red = max_k Σ_{j>p_max−d} |g_{jk}|. PCA-based merging reduces over-approximation volume by factor ∏(σ_k/σ'_k).
claims.paper_ref: Theorem 11 (Reduction error bound), line 1971; Proposition 6 (PCA merging improvement), line 2018

evidence.description: BoundedReduction (line 129) implements generator reduction with Hausdorff error tracking; ReductionErrorCertificate (line 52) records containment proof and error bounds
PCAMerging (line 257) implements PCA-based generator merging with singular value improvement tracking
ReductionChain (line 363) tracks cumulative error across multiple reduction steps with total_error_bound (line 397)
Zonotope.reduce_generators (line 383) and merge_generators (line 395) implement the core reduction operations
test_reduce_generators, test_remove_zero_generators verify reduction correctness
Tests for PCA merging improvement and adaptive reduction

claims.claim: Certificate soundness: if the verification algorithm accepts certificate P = (Z₀, {f♯_ℓ}, C_HB, m*), then the multi-agent system has no interaction race for the specified safety predicate and initial state region.
claims.paper_ref: Proposition 4 (Certificate soundness), line 1793; Definition 8 (Proof certificate), line 1751

evidence.description: Full certificate generation and verification implementation (2390 lines) including CertificateFormat (line 223), certificate building, serialization, and independent checking
CertificateSerializer (line 143) for JSON round-trip; CheckResult (line 116) for verification verdicts
test_valid_certificate_passes, test_hash_check_passes_on_correct, test_tampered_certificate_detected, test_tampered_environment_detected verify certificate integrity checking

test_valid_certificate, test_negative_dual_detected, test_coefficient_mismatch, test_bound_violation, test_round_trip, test_v2_witness_chain_integrity, test_v2_tampered_witness_chain_detected verify v2 certificate format soundness

claims.claim: HB pruning soundness: every concrete state s ∈ γ(Z, C) of a constrained zonotope with HB constraints is reachable under some HB-consistent schedule.

evidence.description: HBConstraint (line 44) and HBConstraintSet (line 177) encode timing constraints ξ_i ≤ ξ_j − δ_min that preserve causal ordering; TimingConstraint (line 112) maps to physical time separation
Constrained zonotope concretization respects generator coefficient constraints
test_consistent_zonotope, test_inconsistent_zonotope, test_partially_consistent verify that HB constraints correctly partition feasible and infeasible schedules
Tests for happens-before graph construction and consistency checking

claims.claim: Assume-guarantee soundness: if for each group g, under assumption Assume_g the group satisfies Guarantee_g, and Guarantee_h ⊆ Assume_g for all h ≠ g, then the full system satisfies ∩_g Guarantee_g.

evidence.description: CompositionRule (line 93) encodes the assume-guarantee rule; SoundnessProof (line 138) constructs inductive proof steps; CircularDependencyResolver (line 177) handles mutual dependencies
Contract specification with Assume/Guarantee pairs for interaction groups
test_linear_contract_creation, test_contract_satisfaction, test_contract_violation, test_spectral_two_groups verify assume-guarantee contracts
Tests for contract-based compositional verification

claims.claim: Group size bound: if the interaction graph has max degree Δ and safety predicates have locality radius r, the maximum interaction group size is O(Δ^r).

evidence.description: InteractionGraphModel (line 99) with from_shared_variables and from_happens_before constructors; component analysis computes group sizes (1473 lines)
InteractionGraph (line 63) computes connected components as interaction groups
test_component_stats, test_interaction_groups, test_max_degree, test_ball_packing_bound, test_predicted_component_size verify group size bounds

claims.claim: Sub-critical percolation: if n agents are uniform in [0,1]² with interaction radius r_n = c√(ln n / n) for c < 1/√π, then with probability 1 − O(1/n), max group size is O(log n).

evidence.description: Percolation threshold analysis with critical density computation and subcritical/supercritical detection
test_critical_density_2d, test_subcritical_detection, test_supercritical_detection, test_expected_degree, test_subcritical_component_bound verify percolation theory

claims.claim: IS consistency: if q(σ) > 0 whenever μ_D(σ) > 0 and σ is in the race set, then p̂_R^IS → p_R a.s. as N → ∞.

evidence.description: Self-normalized IS estimator with support checking; ImportanceWeights (line 37) and ConfidenceInterval (line 109) implement the estimator and convergence monitoring
Proposal distributions with support verification ensuring q > 0 on μ_D support
test_importance_weights, test_effective_sample_size, test_confidence_interval verify IS estimator convergence
test_log_prob_finite, test_hb_constrained, test_uniform_weights_high_ess verify probabilistic estimation

claims.claim: CE convergence: under regularity conditions, the cross-entropy iterates satisfy D_KL(q* || q_t) → 0 at rate O(1/√t).

evidence.description: ParametricProposal (line 42), MultiModalProposal (line 129), and EliteSampleSelector (line 178) implement CE optimization with elite sample fitting (806 lines)
test_elite_selection, test_parametric_proposal, test_proposal_copy verify CE optimization components
test_construction, test_sample_returns_schedules verify parametric proposal convergence

claims.claim: All detection configurations complete in under 0.52 seconds (single-threaded). The largest configuration (8 warehouse agents) requires 0.519s.

evidence.description: Timing data for all configurations showing sub-second analysis times
Timing infrastructure for measuring wall-clock analysis time
Experiment runner with timing collection

claims.claim: Warehouse FPR increases with agent count (0.83 for 4 agents to 0.96 for 8 agents) due to zonotope wrapping effect in higher dimensions.
claims.paper_ref: Table 1, line 639; Section 6.1 (False positive trade-off), line 667

evidence.description: warehouse_corridor results showing FPR progression across agent counts
False positive rate analysis and sensitivity computation
test_false_positive_estimator, test_sensitivity_analysis verify FP analysis

claims.claim: Multi-agent execution definition: a tuple M = (A, S, {π_i}, T, Σ) with agents, joint state space, policies, transition function, and schedules.

evidence.description: Base environment class defining agents, state space, policies, transition function, and schedule sets
Concrete highway intersection environment instantiating the definition
Concrete warehouse corridor environment instantiating the definition
Tests verifying environment structure matches the formal definition

claims.claim: Zonotope definition: Z = {c + Σ ξ_j g_j | ξ_j ∈ [-1,1]} with center c and generators g_1,...,g_p. Constrained zonotope extends with linear constraints on generator coefficients.

evidence.description: Zonotope dataclass (line 91) with center, generators, bounding_box, affine_transform, join, widening, contains_point, sample, minkowski_sum (712 lines)
HBConstraintSet (line 177) adds linear constraints on generator coefficients for the constrained zonotope extension
Comprehensive zonotope tests: test_basic_creation, test_bounding_box, test_affine_transform, test_join, test_widening, test_contains_center, test_minkowski_sum

claims.claim: Safety margin definition: m(σ) = min_k (h_k − H_k · reach(σ,s₀)) / ||H_k||. A race is detected when MCTS finds σ₁, σ₂ with m(σ₁) > 0 > m(σ₂).

evidence.description: MCTS search (1003 lines) guided by safety margin minimization with UCB1 and HB-consistency pruning
Linear safety predicates in Hs ≤ h form with margin computation
test_ucb1_safety, test_safety_margin_pruner verify safety margin-guided search

summary.notes: C4 (PSPACE-hardness) is partially grounded because the full reduction proof is purely mathematical and not directly implementable as code; the paper proof sketch references bounded one-counter automata. All other claims have direct implementation artifacts, tests, and/or experimental results.
